prevalence.value: <0.01% annual incidence (mostly travel-related; occasional local outbreaks in southern EU)
prevalence.trend: increasing risk of autochthonous transmission in parts of southern Europe due to Aedes expansion

burden.mortalityRate: very low; severe cases mainly in older travelers/comorbidities

riskFactors: Aedes aegypti/albopictus presence in southern EU
vulnerablePopulations: communities in areas with established Aedes vectors
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical suspicion with travel history; confirmation by PCR/NS1 (early) or IgM/IgG serology; case classification per ECDC guidance

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; travel-related fever evaluation common
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with supportive care; hospitalizations mainly in severe cases

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Broad laboratory capacity for PCR/serology in reference networks; mandatory reporting in many countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care and monitoring; vector control and public health response for local transmission events
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Emerging local transmission risk in southern EU and the need for rapid vector control during outbreaks
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong surveillance and outbreak response capacity, and wide access to diagnostics in most member states
